OIIX A3714/25.explanation: This is an important message for foreign airlines and aircraft flying to, from, or through Iran. It states that they must now send their flight plans and any updates to a specific address (OIIXIFPS). Also, all international flights entering Iran's airspace or landing at Iranian airports must follow new procedures detailed in Iran's aviation rulebook.

OIIX A3715/25.explanation: This is an aviation alert for the Tehran airspace. Several specific high-frequency radio channels (5658 KHZ, 5667 KHZ, 6925 KHZ, 8091 KHZ, 8918 KHZ, 10018 KHZ, 13288 KHZ, and 13312 KHZ) used for communication with Tehran Information are currently not working. This affects all aircraft flying in the Tehran Flight Information Region. The issue started on December 11, 2025, and is expected to continue until March 11, 2026.

OIIX A3716/25.explanation: A new controlled airspace has been established south of Tehran. This means that from about 19,500 feet upwards, aircraft flying in this specific area will be under the control of Tehran Radar. Pilots will need to communicate on frequency 133.400 MHz in English or Persian. This change is permanent and helps manage air traffic in the region.

OIIX A3717/25.explanation: This notice informs pilots that several radio frequencies used for air traffic control communication in the Tehran region (OIIX) are changing. Pilots flying in this area will need to use the new frequency, 258.400 MHz, for communication in various sectors. This change is active from December 11, 2025, to March 11, 2026.

OIIX A3718/25.explanation: This is an official notice for pilots flying in Iran. It states that several specific air routes (like R659, G666, etc.) can now be flown using a more modern and efficient navigation method called RNAV5. This means aircraft with the right equipment can use their onboard systems to fly these routes more directly, starting from the lowest safe altitude for each route. This change is active from December 11, 2025, until March 11, 2026.

OIIX A3810/25.explanation: A radar system in the Tehran region of Iran is broken and will be out of service from December 2025 to March 2026. This means air traffic controllers might not be able to see planes on their radar screens in certain parts of the sky, specifically in some areas where planes fly between cities. Pilots flying through these areas need to be aware that radar services might not be available, which could affect how air traffic is managed.

OIIX B0995/25.explanation: This is an update about specific, required flight paths for domestic planes flying between two airports (OIII and OIMD) within Iran's airspace. Planes must now follow these exact routes.

OIIX A0064/26.explanation: Attention pilots! A specific danger zone called OID125 in the Tehran region is active and should be avoided by all aircraft. This zone is active daily from 4:30 AM to 6:30 PM UTC, starting January 5, 2026, and ending March 19, 2026. The restricted area extends from the ground up to 18,000 feet above sea level.

OIIX A0066/26.explanation: An area in Iran, specifically designated as OID127, will be off-limits for aircraft daily from January 5th to March 20th, 2026. This restriction is in effect between 03:30 AM and 07:30 PM UTC each day, from the ground up to 12,000 feet above sea level. Pilots flying in the region must be aware of this activated restricted zone.

OIIX A0070/26.explanation: An area of airspace near Tehran (called OID122) will be restricted for aircraft from January 5 to March 19, 2026. During this period, the restriction will be active daily from 02:30 AM to 08:30 PM UTC, from the ground up to 16,000 feet. This means planes cannot fly through this specific area during these times.

OIIX A0072/26.explanation: An area in the sky over Iran, known as OID123, will be dangerous for aircraft to fly through. This 'Danger Area' will be active daily from early morning until evening, starting January 5, 2026, and lasting until March 19, 2026. It covers the airspace from the ground up to 18,000 feet. Pilots should avoid this area because dangerous activities are taking place there.

OIIX A0065/26.explanation: Attention pilots and air traffic! There will be gun firing exercises taking place in a specific area within Iran's airspace. This activity will occur from January 8, 2026, until March 19, 2026, specifically on Saturdays and Thursdays between 11:30 AM and 2:30 PM UTC. The affected area extends from the ground up to 18,000 feet above sea level. Aircraft should be aware of this potential danger during the specified times.

OIIX A0080/26.explanation: This is an alert for pilots flying in Iranian airspace. From January 8 to March 19, 2026, on Saturdays and Thursdays between 11:30 AM and 2:30 PM UTC, the lowest safe altitude for flying on specific routes (called airways M434 and Z740) between two points named ASLAX and NOLTO has been increased to 19,000 feet. This means planes must fly at or above 19,000 feet in that area during those times.

OIIX B0031/26.explanation: An area in Iran, specifically designated OID124, is restricted for all aircraft flights (both instrument and visual rules) daily from 03:30 to 18:30 UTC. This restriction is in effect from January 12, 2026, until March 19, 2026, and applies from the ground up to 13,000 feet above sea level. Pilots must avoid this area during the specified times.

OIIX A0198/26.explanation: This is an update to the flight rules and procedures within Iran's airspace (Tehran FIR). It specifically changes the routes and takeoff/landing paths (called SIDs and STARs) for several domestic flights between certain airports, like from Kerman to Mashhad, and from Shiraz to Bandar Abbas, Kish Island, and Chah Bahar. These changes are part of a larger plan to manage air traffic and are detailed in an aviation manual.

OIIX B0038/26.explanation: Attention all pilots and air traffic! From January 13, 2026, to March 19, 2026, there will be daily gun firing exercises in a specific area within the Tehran region of Iran. These exercises will occur every day between 03:30 AM and 06:30 PM UTC. The affected airspace extends from the ground up to 14,000 feet above sea level. Aircraft should be aware of this activity in the specified coordinates.

OIIX A0205/26.explanation: This is an aviation warning about gun firing exercises taking place in a specific area within the Mashhad region of Iran. The exercises will occur daily from January 13, 2026, to March 19, 2026, between 03:30 and 19:30 UTC. The affected airspace extends from the ground up to 20,000 feet above sea level. Aircraft should be aware of this activity in the specified coordinates.

OIIX B0039/26.explanation: Attention pilots and anyone flying in the airspace over a specific area in Iran! From January 13, 2026, to March 19, 2026, there will be daily gun firing exercises. These exercises will take place between 03:30 AM and 06:30 PM UTC (Coordinated Universal Time) each day. The affected area is a specific rectangular zone defined by coordinates, and the activity will occur from the ground up to 13,000 feet above sea level. All aircraft, regardless of their flight rules, should be aware of this military activity.

OIIX A0206/26.explanation: An area in the Tehran region, specifically designated as OID130, is now restricted for all aircraft. This means planes cannot fly through this airspace from the ground up to 13,000 feet. The restriction is active daily from 05:30 AM to 11:30 AM UTC, starting January 13, 2026, and ending March 19, 2026.

OIIX B0040/26.explanation: This is an official alert for pilots about gun firing exercises taking place in a specific area of Iran. The exercises will happen daily from January 13 to March 19, 2026, between 03:30 AM and 06:30 PM UTC. Aircraft should be aware that this activity will occur from the ground up to 15,000 feet above sea level in the defined area.

OIIX A0207/26.explanation: Pilots and aircraft should be aware that there will be gun firing in a specific area within the Mashhad region of Iran. This activity will take place daily from January 13, 2026, until March 19, 2026, specifically between 03:30 AM and 08:30 PM UTC each day. The affected airspace extends from the ground up to 20,000 feet above sea level. Aircraft should avoid this area during these times due to the danger.

OIIX A0208/26.explanation: An area called OID128 in the Tehran region will be active and restricted for aircraft from January 13 to March 19, 2026. This means planes cannot fly there during specific hours each day (between 2:30 AM and 4:30 PM UTC), from the ground up to 15,000 feet. It's a warning for pilots.

OIIX B0041/26.explanation: This is a warning for pilots that there will be gun firing in a specific area near the coordinates 34 degrees 57 minutes North, 59 degrees 13 minutes East. This activity will take place from January 13, 2026, to March 19, 2026, daily between 3:30 AM and 6:30 PM UTC. The gun firing will occur from the ground up to 11,000 feet above sea level.

OIIX A0229/26.explanation: Pilots flying in the Tehran region need to be aware that a specific area, called OID121, is now a restricted zone. This means aircraft are not allowed to fly there without special permission. This restriction is in effect from January 15 to March 19, 2026, and applies daily between 3:30 AM and 7:30 PM UTC. The restricted space goes from the ground up to 30,000 feet high.

OIIX B0062/26.explanation: Starting January 25, 2026, and lasting for a few months, all private planes and planes flying by visual rules (VFR) are not allowed to fly in Iran. The only exceptions are flights for hospitals, search and rescue, government, military, and specific oil company helicopters, but even these need special permission beforehand. Flights operating within airport traffic zones are also exempt.

OIIX B0081/26.explanation: This is a warning for pilots about gun firing exercises happening in a specific area in Iran. These exercises will take place daily from January 27, 2026, until March 19, 2026, between 3:30 AM and 6:30 PM UTC. The activity will occur from the ground up to 14,000 feet above sea level. Pilots flying in this region should be aware of this activity during these times.

OIIX A0367/26.explanation: An area called OID73 in the Tehran region will be restricted for aircraft. This means planes cannot fly there from the ground up to 11,500 feet. This restriction will be in effect daily from January 27, 2026, to March 20, 2026, between 3:30 AM and 7:30 PM UTC.

OIIX A0386/26.explanation: An area called OID84 will be restricted for aircraft from January 31 to March 19, 2026. This restriction will be in effect daily between 3:30 AM and 7:30 PM UTC, from the ground up to 13,000 feet above sea level. Pilots should avoid this area during these times.

OIIX A0516/26.explanation: An area in Iran, identified as OID126, will be a restricted no-fly zone daily from February 19 to March 19, 2026. During these dates, no aircraft can fly in this area between 03:30 AM and 04:30 PM UTC, from the ground up to 14,000 feet.

OIIX A0671/26.explanation: A crucial radio beacon called ANK VOR, which helps aircraft navigate, is currently broken and cannot be used. It will be out of service from February 23, 2026, until at least May 24, 2026. Pilots flying in the area will need to use alternative navigation methods. Once it's repaired, it will undergo a flight test to ensure it's working correctly.

OIIX B0174/26.explanation: This is a warning for pilots about drone activity (Remotely Piloted Aircraft) taking place in a specific area. The activity will occur from February 25 to March 20, 2026, from the ground up to 10,000 feet above sea level. Pilots should be aware of this drone presence in the specified region.

OIIX A0712/26.explanation: An important air route, called N39, in Tehran's airspace (between DEMBA and OBRIX) will be closed for all planes flying through that area. This closure will last from February 28 to May 29, 2026. Planes flying west will need to use an alternative route through RADAL, IMKER, and ULDUS, flying no lower than 27,500 feet. Planes flying east will need to use an alternative route through ULDUS, ALKUP, IMLIM, OXADU, and OBRIX, flying no lower than 28,500 feet.

OIIX A0713/26.explanation: An air highway (called G208/L125) in the airspace over Tehran, Iran, is temporarily closed for planes that are just passing through the area. If a pilot planned to use this specific route, they now need to fly a different, specified path (via ROVAD direct to IKA). However, planes arriving at Tehran's main airports (Imam Khomeini, Mehrabad, and Payam) can still use a specific entry point (RADAL). This change is in effect from February 28 to May 10, 2026.

OIIX A0724/26.explanation: This is an update for pilots flying in the Tehran region. It provides new, additional phone numbers (a direct line and a mobile number) to contact Air Traffic Control.

OIIX B0180/26.explanation: This is an administrative message for pilots and aviation staff. It's a checklist of important aviation documents (like updates to flight rules and information circulars) that they should have. It lists specific document numbers for the years 2022, 2025, and 2026, along with the latest updates to the Aeronautical Information Publication (AIP). It also notes that there won't be a specific type of update published on March 19, 2026, and mentions a particular NOTAM class that is currently valid. Essentially, it's a reminder to ensure all their aviation manuals and documents are up-to-date.

OIIX A0727/26.explanation: This is a routine update for pilots and aviation personnel in the Tehran flight region. It provides a comprehensive list of all currently active notices, updates, and important documents they need to review to ensure they have the most current information for safe flights. It also includes details about upcoming publication schedules for aviation information and a specific note about an older, valid notice.

OIIX A0728/26.explanation: The airspace over Tehran, Iran, is completely closed to all flights from March 14 to March 22, 2026. Only emergency flights (like medical or search and rescue), government, military flights, or those with special permission from the aviation authority are allowed to fly during this period.

OIIX A0729/26.explanation: This is a warning for all flights in the Persian Gulf and Oman Sea, within the airspace managed by Tehran. It states that there might be an expansion of a dangerous or conflict zone into international waters due to security reasons. Pilots should be aware and exercise caution when flying in this area between March 14 and March 22, 2026.

OIIX A0357/26.explanation: This is an announcement for pilots and aviation professionals. It means that on March 19, 2026, there will be no new updates or changes to the official aviation documents (like maps and procedures) that are usually released on that date. So, the existing information remains current.

KICZ A0016/20.explanation: The U.S. government is warning American airlines, pilots, and aircraft operators to be very careful when flying over the Persian Gulf and Gulf of Oman. This is due to ongoing military activity and political tensions in the region. They should review security information, avoid routes close to Iranian airspace to prevent being mistaken for something else by air defense systems, and be prepared for potential GPS and communication jamming. They must also report any safety or security incidents. A separate, stricter warning that bans U.S. flights over Iranian airspace is still in effect.

KICZ A0011/24.explanation: The United States government has issued a new rule (SFAR 117) that bans certain US flights from flying in the airspace over Tehran, Iran. This ban started on October 3, 2024, and is expected to last until October 31, 2027. All pilots and airlines are required to read the full details of this rule.
